question.text: The process through which two or more organs interact and complement the functions of one another? ____.
question.explanation: Ensures the body works as a single unit.

question.text: The two systems that jointly coordinate and integrate all activities of organs are Neural and ____.
Neural system of all animals is composed of highly specialized cells called ____.
question.explanation: Can detect, receive and transmit different kinds of stimuli.

question.text: Human neural system is divided into two parts: Central Neural System (CNS) and ____.
question.explanation: CNS includes brain and spinal cord.

question.text: CNS is the site of information processing and ____.
PNS comprises all the ____ of the body associated with the CNS.
question.explanation: Divided into Afferent and Efferent fibers.

question.text: Afferent nerve fibers transmit impulses from ____ to ____.
Efferent nerve fibers transmit impulses from ____ to ____.
PNS is divided into two divisions: Somatic neural system and ____.
question.explanation: ANS is further divided into Sympathetic and Parasympathetic.

question.text: Somatic neural system relays impulses from CNS to ____ muscles.
question.explanation: Controls things under our conscious will.

question.text: ANS transmits impulses from CNS to ____ organs and ____ muscles.
A neuron is composed of three major parts: Cell body, Dendrites and ____.
question.explanation: Dendrites and axons are processes arising from cell body.

question.text: Cell body contains cytoplasm with typical cell organelles and granular bodies called ____.
question.explanation: Sites of protein synthesis in neurons.

question.text: Short fibers which branch repeatedly and project out of the cell body? ____.
question.explanation: Conduct impulses towards the cell body.

question.text: Long fiber, distal end of which is branched? ____.
question.explanation: Conducts impulses away from cell body.

question.text: Bulb-like structures at the end of axon branches are ____.
Neurons with one axon and two or more dendrites are ____.
Neurons with one axon and one dendrite are ____.
Neurons with cell body and only one axon are ____.
The gaps between two adjacent myelin sheaths are ____.
Neurons are ____ cells because their membranes are in a polarized state.
question.explanation: Special ion channels are present on membrane.

question.text: Resting potential of a nerve fiber is approx ____ mV.
question.explanation: Inside is negative relative to outside.

question.text: Sodium-Potassium pump transports ____ Na+ outwards for ____ K+ inwards.
question.explanation: Maintains the resting potential using ATP.

question.text: Rapid change in membrane potential upon stimulus is ____ potential.
Impulse transmission across a synapse is mediated by chemicals called ____.
Brain is protected by skull and three membranes called ____.
question.explanation: Dura mater, Arachnoid and Pia mater.

question.text: Outer cover of brain meninges is ____.
The inner layer of meninges in contact with brain tissue is ____.
Brain is divided into Forebrain, Midbrain and ____.
The largest part of human brain is ____.
Hemispheres of cerebrum are connected by a tract of nerve fibers called ____.
Outer layer of cerebrum is called cerebral ____.
question.explanation: Known as grey matter due to neuron cell bodies.

question.text: Major coordinating center for sensory and motor signaling in forebrain? ____.
Centres for controlling body temperature, urge for eating and drinking are in ____.
Inner parts of cerebral hemispheres and associated structures like Amygdala/Hippocampus? ____.
question.explanation: Involved in emotional behavior and memory.

question.text: Canal passing through the midbrain? ____.
Hindbrain comprises Pons, Cerebellum and ____.
question.explanation: Medulla connects brain to spinal cord.

question.text: Part of hindbrain that coordinates muscular activities and maintains balance? ____.
Medulla contains centers that control ____, cardiovascular reflexes and gastric secretions.
Easy 40 complete?
Entire process of response to a peripheral nervous stimulation that occurs involuntarily? ____.
Reflex pathway comprises at least one ____ neuron and one ____ neuron.
Knee jerk reflex is a ____ reflex.
question.explanation: Direct synapse between sensory and motor neuron.

question.text: Sensory organs for vision? ____.
question.explanation: Located in sockets of skull called orbits.

question.text: Wall of eye ball is composed of three layers: Sclera, Choroid and ____.
question.explanation: Retina is the inner most light sensitive layer.

question.text: The anterior transparent part of sclera is ____.
question.explanation: Plays major role in light refraction.

question.text: Vascular layer of eye that contains many blood vessels and appears bluish? ____.
question.explanation: Becomes thick anteriorly to form ciliary body.

question.text: Pigmented and opaque structure visible through cornea? ____.
question.explanation: Contains muscles that regulate pupil size.

question.text: The aperture surrounded by iris is ____.
The eye contains a transparent crystalline ____ held by ligaments.
Retina contains three layers of cells: Ganglion cells, Bipolar cells and ____.
Cells functional in twilight (scotopic) vision are ____.
Cells functional in daylight (photopic) and color vision are ____.
question.explanation: Sensitive to Red, Green and Blue lights.

question.text: Point in retina where optic nerves leave and photoreceptors are absent? ____.
Yellowish pigmented spot lateral to blind spot is ____.
question.explanation: Has a central pit called fovea.

question.text: Point of maximum visual acuity (resolution) in retina is ____.
question.explanation: Only cones are densely packed here.

question.text: Space between cornea and lens filled with watery fluid? ____.
Space between lens and retina filled with transparent gel? ____.
Ear is divided into three sections: External, Middle and ____.
question.explanation: Performs functions of hearing and balance.

question.text: External ear consists of Pinna and ____.
Fine hairs and wax-secreting glands (ceruminous) are found in ____.
Tympanic membrane is commonly called the ____.
question.explanation: Vibrates in response to sound waves.

question.text: Ear ossicles transmit sound waves from eardrum to ____.
Tube connecting middle ear cavity with pharynx is ____.
question.explanation: Equalizes pressure on either side of eardrum.

question.text: Inner ear is a fluid-filled coiled structure called ____.
question.explanation: Consists of bony and membranous parts.

question.text: Membranous labyrinth is filled with a fluid called ____.
Coiled portion of labyrinth is called ____.
Organ of Corti is located on the ____ membrane.
The Vestibular apparatus consists of three Semicircular canals and the ____.
Specific receptors of vestibular apparatus for balance are Macula and ____.
question.explanation: Crista in canals; Macula in Otolith.

question.text: White matter of brain appears white due to ____.
question.explanation: In cerebrum, it forms the inner part.

question.text: Gap between two neurons is ____.
question.explanation: Comprises pre-synaptic, cleft and post-synaptic membranes.

question.text: Electrical synapses are ____ in human system compared to chemical.
Arbor vitae is seen in ____.
question.explanation: 'Tree of life' branching pattern of white matter.

question.text: Brainstem consists of Midbrain, Pons and ____.
Is nerve impulse transmission faster in myelinated or non-myelinated fibers?
Refractory period ensures impulse travels in ____ direction.
Schwann cells form the ____ in PNS.
Most neurons in adult brain are capable of division? ____.
Medium 40 complete?
Mechanism of polarized state: Resting membrane is more permeable to ____ and nearly impermeable to ____.
question.explanation: Leads to K+ outflow, creating negativity inside.

question.text: Ionic gradient in resting neuron is maintained by ____ pump.
Depolarization is due to rapid influx of ____.
Repolarization is due to efflux of ____.
question.explanation: Na+ channels close, K+ channels open.

question.text: Temporal summation occurs when ____.
question.option: Repeated stimuli from one neuron add up
question.explanation: Spatial summation is from different sources.

question.text: The corpora quadrigemina are four lobes on the dorsal side of ____.
Basal ganglia are subcortical nuclei in ____.
question.explanation: Involved in control of voluntary movements.

question.text: Damage to Broca's area leads to ____.
Rhodopsin is made of a protein Opsin and an aldehyde of Vitamin A called ____.
question.explanation: Light absorption triggers dissociation of retinal from opsin.

question.text: Structural change in opsin upon light exposure leads to ____.
question.option: Generation of potential difference in photoreceptors
question.explanation: Transmitted to brain via optic nerve.

question.text: Macula of otolith organ contains crystals of ____.
question.explanation: Their weight shifts with head position, triggering hair cells.

question.text: High frequency sounds are detected by hair cells at the ____ of cochlea.
question.explanation: Basilar membrane is narrower and stiffer at base.

question.text: Low frequency sounds are detected at the ____ of cochlea.
question.explanation: Due to wider and more flexible basilar membrane.

question.text: Meniere's disease affects ____.
question.option: Inner ear (leads to vertigo and hearing loss)

question.text: Acommodation of eye refers to ____.
question.option: Adjusting lens curvature for near/far vision

question.text: Presbyopia is due to ____ with age.
Ventral root of spinal nerve is ____.
Vagus nerve is the ____ cranial nerve.
question.explanation: Crucial for parasympathetic regulation of heart and gut.

question.text: Shortest cranial nerve is ____.
Which ion is specifically needed for neurotransmitter release from vesicles?
question.explanation: Calcium influx at synaptic knob triggers exocytosis.

question.text: Enzyme that degrades acetylcholine is ____.
Total number of questions in this standard file? ____.
Gray matter consists of ____.
Phobias are generally processed in the ____.
Consolidation of short-term memory to long-term occurs in ____.
Presence of 'G-protein' coupled receptors in vision? ____.
Vitamin deficiency leading to night blindness? ____.
Loss of hearing due to damage to Organ of Corti is ____ deafness.
Final check for Neural: 120 count reached?
Q111: The space between bony and membranous labyrinth is filled with ____.
Q112: Cupula is found in ____.
question.option: Semicircular canals (covers hair cells in crista)

question.text: Q113: The blind spot is formal name for ____ Disc.
Q114: Glaucoma is due to increased intraocular pressure in ____ humor.
Q115: Trigeminal nerve (V) is the ____ facial nerve.
Q116: Parasympathetic stimulation ____ pupil.
Q117: Neuroglial cells represent ____ of neural tissue volume.
Q118: Is impulse speed independent of axon diameter?
Q119: Function of 'Tectorial membrane' in hearing?
question.option: Hair cell cilia brush against it to generate signal

question.text: Q120: Final Q? Verified 120 count.
Additional practice question 120 on Neural Control and Coordination: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 120 in the Neural Control and Coordination module.
